rulings.Case Title: MILAN BACOKA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 14, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Flowserve Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bacoka was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Bacoka¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s written contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not meet its burden of production under the other prong of Aguilar because Mr. Adams¿ declaration does not affirmatively negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production to make substantial factor causation an issue. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Ms. Ringo¿s testimony are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MILAN BACOKA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 14, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 3 (punitive damages) and off calendar as to issues 1 and 2 (strict liability and false representation). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs have dismissed their causes of action for strict liability and false representation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MILAN BACOKA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 14, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Goulds Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. As to the ¿OEM¿ issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bacoka was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Bacoka¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on that issue. As to asbestos content, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the products or materials attributable to defendant to which Mr. Bacoka was exposed (packing and gaskets) contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Bradshaw¿s deposition testimony and to Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
